mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3
additional_notes: Approved for HCC/RCC; FLT3-ITD AML often shows low-nM sensitivity.

mechanism: Inhibits ABL and FLT3 kinases
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; frequently low-nM in FLT3-ITD lines (including MOLM13).

mechanism: Inhibits MET, FLT3, and VEGFR kinases
additional_notes: Approved for RCC/thyroid; reported activity in FLT3-ITD AML at low–mid nM.

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and VEGFR kinases
additional_notes: Approved for RCC; commonly tens of nM in FLT3-ITD AML.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3
additional_notes: Approved for CRC/HCC; typically mid‑nM to sub‑µM in FLT3-ITD contexts.

mechanism: Inhibits Src and ABL; some FLT3 inhibition
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; often tens of nM to sub‑µM in AML.

category: EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor; also FLT3 inhibitor
mechanism: Inhibits EGFR and FLT3 (including D835 mutants)
additional_notes: Approved for NSCLC; frequently ~10–100 nM in FLT3‑ITD AML (MOLM13/MV4‑11).

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 to block MAPK signaling
additional_notes: Approved for melanoma; RAS/MAPK‑driven AML lines show ~10–300 nM single‑agent sensitivity; often synergizes with FLT3 inhibitors.

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1 signaling
additional_notes: Approved for RCC; AML lines commonly show 10–300 nM growth inhibition, context‑dependent.

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome to disrupt proteostasis
additional_notes: Approved for myeloma/MCL; many AML lines respond in the 5–50 nM range.

mechanism: Inhibits nuclear export protein XPO1 (CRM1)
additional_notes: Approved for myeloma/DLBCL; AML lines (including MOLM13) often 10–200 nM.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase causing redox stress
additional_notes: Approved for rheumatoid arthritis; AML IC50s frequently 0.3–3 µM.

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial protein synthesis
additional_notes: AML blasts/lines often 1–20 µM; targets leukemia stem cell metabolism.

mechanism: STAT5 pathway inhibition
additional_notes: FLT3‑ITD AML (STAT5‑dependent) typically 1–10 µM.

mechanism: Dopamine receptor antagonist with ion channel effects; LSC‑active
additional_notes: Often low‑ to mid‑µM in AML.

mechanism: Affects microtubules/other
additional_notes: AML IC50s commonly ~0.1–5 µM.

mechanism: Modulates oxidative phosphorylation and Wnt/STAT pathways
additional_notes: Many leukemia models ~0.1–2 µM.

mechanism: Lysosomal permeabilization
additional_notes: AML lines including LSCs often 1–10 µM.

mechanism: Induces proteostasis/ER stress and affects Akt signaling
additional_notes: AML IC50s typically 3–15 µM.

mechanism: Iron chelation and inhibition of EIF5A hypusination
additional_notes: Leukemia IC50s commonly 0.5–5 µM.
